title: Wealth.
content:
В В В В [From Arthur Selwyn's Note-book.]


В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Here in The City I ponder,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Through its long pathways I wander.
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В These are the spires that were gleaming
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В All through my juvenile dreaming.
В В В В В В В В В В В В This is The Something I heard, far away,
В В В В В В В В В В В В When, at the close of a tired Summer day,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Resting from work on the lap of a lawn,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Gazing to whither The Sun-god had gone,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Leaving behind him his mantles of gold - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В This is The Something by which I was told;
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В "Bend your head, dreamer, and listen - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Come to my splendors that glisten!
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Either to triumph they call you,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Or to what worst could befall you!"
В В В В В В В В В В В В This is The Something that thrilled my desires,
В В В В В В В В В В В В When the weird Morning had kindled his fires,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And the gray city of clouds in the east
В В В В В В В В В В В В Lighted its streets as for pageant or feast,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Whisp'ring - my spirit elating - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В "Come to me, boy, I am waiting!
В В В В В В В В В В В В Bring me your muscle and spirit and brain - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В Here to my glory-strewn, ruin-strewn plain!"
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Treading the trough of the furrow,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Digging where life-rootlets burrow,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Blade of the food-harvest swinging,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В In the barns toiling and singing,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Breath of a hay-meadow smelling,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Forest-trees loving and felling - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Where'er my spirit was turning,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Lived that mysterious yearning!
В В В В В В В В В В В В When in the old country school-house I conned
В В В В В В В В В В В В Legends of life in the broad world beyond - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В When in the trim hamlet-college I cast
В В В В В В В В В В В В Wondering glances at days that were past - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В Ever I longed for the walls and the streets,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And the rich conflict that energy meets!

В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *

В В В В В В В В В В В В So I have come: but The City is great
В В В В В В В В В В В В Bearing me down like a brute with its weight.
В В В В В В В В В В В В So I have come: but The City is cold,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And I am lonelier now than of old.

В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *

В В В В В В В В В В В В Yet, 'tis the same restless story:
В В В В В В В В В В В В Even to fail here were glory!
В В В В В В В В В В В В Grand, to be part of this ocean
В В В В В В В В В В В В Of matter and mind and emotion!
В В В В В В В В В В В В Here flow the streams of endeavor,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Cityward trending forever. - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В Wheat-stalks that tassel the field,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Harvests of opulent yield,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Grass-blades that fence with each other,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Flower-blossoms - sister and brother - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В Roots that are sturdy and tender,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Stalks in your thrift and your splendor,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Mind that is fertile and daring,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Face that true beauty is wearing - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В All that is strongest and fleetest,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В All that are dainty and sweetest.
В В В В В В В В В В В В Look to the domes and the glittering spires,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Waiting for you with majestic desires!
В В В В В В В В В В В В List to The City's gaunt, thunderous roar,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Calling and calling for you evermore!
В В В В В В В В В В В В Long in the fields you may labor and wait - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В You and your tribe may come early or late;
В В В В В В В В В В В В Beauty and excellence dwell and will dwell
В В В В В В В В В В В В Oft amid garden and moorland and fell;
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Long generations may hold them,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Centuries oft can enfold them;
В В В В В В В В В В В В But the rich City's they some time shall be,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Sure as the spring is the food of the sea.

В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *


В В В В [From Farmer Harrington's Calendar.]

В В В В SEPTEMBER 20, 18 - .

В В В В В В В В В В В В Wind in the south-west; weather wondrous fine;
В В В В В В В В В В В В Thermometer 'twixt seventy-eight and nine.
В В В В В В В В В В В В Ground rather dry; sun flails us over-warm;
В В В В В В В В В В В В It's most time for the equinoctial storm.
В В В В В В В В В В В В Family healthy as could be desired;
В В В В В В В В В В В В Except we're somewhat mind and body tired
В В В В В В В В В В В В At moving over such a lengthy road,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And settling down here in our town abode,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And wrestling with the pains that filter through one
В В В В В В В В В В В В When he gives up an old home for a new one.

В В В В В В В В В В В В Old Calendar, you've always stood me true;
В В В В В В В В В В В В Now I'll change works, and do the same by you!
В В В В В В В В В В В В You're just as good as when, with aching arm,
В В В В В В В В В В В В I cleared and worked that eighty-acre farm!
В В В В В В В В В В В В And every night, in those hard, dear old days,
В В В В В В В В В В В В 'Twas one of my most unconditional ways,
В В В В В В В В В В В В When to my labors I had said Good-night,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And recompensed my home-made appetite,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And talked with Wife, and traded family views,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And gathered all the latest township news,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And dealt my sons a sly fraternal hit,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And flirted with my daughters just a bit,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And through the papers tried my way to see,
В В В В В В В В В В В В So the world shouldn't slip out from under me,
В В В В В В В В В В В В As I was saying - in those sweet old days,
В В В В В В В В В В В В 'Twas one of my most unconditional ways,
В В В В В В В В В В В В To go to you, old book, before I'd sleep,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And hand you over all the day to keep.

В В В В В В В В В В В В I gave you up what weather I could find,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Likewise the different phases of my mind;
В В В В В В В В В В В В What my hard hands from morn to night had done,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And what my mind had been subsisting on;
В В В В В В В В В В В В What accidents had touched my brain with doubt,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And what successes it had whittled out;
В В В В В В В В В В В В How well I had been able to control
В В В В В В В В В В В В The weather fluctuations of my soul;
В В В В В В В В В В В В What progress or what failures I had made
В В В В В В В В В В В В In spying round and stealing Nature's trade;
В В В В В В В В В В В В The seeds of actions planted long ago,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And whether they had blossomed out or no;
В В В В В В В В В В В В And oft, from what you of the past could tell,
В В В В В В В В В В В В I've learned to steer my future pretty well.

В В В В В В В В В В В В And now I'M RICH (who ever thought 'twould be!)
В В В В В В В В В В В В I'll stand by you, as you have stood by me;
В В В В В В В В В В В В And now I'm "City people" - having moved
В В В В В В В В В В В В (My circumstances suddenly improved)
В В В В В В В В В В В В Into this town, with some quick-gotten pelf,
В В В В В В В В В В В В To educate my children and myself,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And give my wife, who has a pedigree,
В В В В В В В В В В В В A chance to flutter round her family tree,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And show her natural city airs and graces
В В В В В В В В В В В В (Which didn't "take" quite so well in country places)
В В В В В В В В В В В В Now we are here, old fellow, while we stay
В В В В В В В В В В В В I'll give you all the news from day to day.
В В В В В В В В В В В В I'll find the good that in this city lurks,
В В В В В В В В В В В В By regular, systematic, hard days' works;
В В В В В В В В В В В В I'll rummage fearless round amongst the harm,
В В В В В В В В В В В В As when I hoed up thistles on my farm;
В В В В В В В В В В В В Shake hands with Virtue, help Sin while I spurn it,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And if there's anything to learn, I'll learn it.

В В В В В В В В В В В В How little I suspected, by the way - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В Scrambling for pennies in that patch of clay,
В В В В В В В В В В В В The bare expenses of our lives to meet - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В That waves of wealth were washing at my feet!
В В В В В В В В В В В В And when my hard and rather lazy soil
В В В В В В В В В В В В Sprung a leak upward with petroleum-oil - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В When, through the wonder in my glad old eyes,
В В В В В В В В В В В В I saw tall derricks by the hundred rise,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Flinging wealth at me with unceasing hand,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And turning to a mine my hard old land,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Until it seemed as if the spell would hold
В В В В В В В В В В В В Till every blade of grass was turned to gold - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В I felt, as never yet had come to me,
В В В В В В В В В В В В How little round the curves of life we see;
В В В В В В В В В В В В Or, in our rushings on, suspect or view
В В В В В В В В В В В В What sort of stations we are coming to!
В В В В В В В В В В В В It brought a similar twinge - though not so bad - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В As once, when losing every cent I had.

В В В В В В В В В В В В But still it could not shift my general views;
В В В В В В В В В В В В My mind didn't faint at one good piece of news.
В В В В В В В В В В В В I think I'd too much ballast 'neath my sail
В В В В В В В В В В В В To be capsized by one good prosperous gale
В В В В В В В В В В В В (Same as I didn't lie down and give up all
В В В В В В В В В В В В That other time, when tipped up by a squall).
В В В В В В В В В В В В I didn't go spreeing for my money's sake,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Or with my business matters lie awake;
В В В В В В В В В В В В 'Twould never do, as I informed my wife,
В В В В В В В В В В В В To let a little money spoil our life!

В В В В В В В В В В В В And now I'm rich (who ever thought 'twould be!)
В В В В В В В В В В В В I'll look about, and see what I can see;
В В В В В В В В В В В В Appoint myself a visiting committee,
В В В В В В В В В В В В With power to act in all parts of the city;
В В В В В В В В В В В В Growl when I must, commend whene'er I can,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And lose no chance to help my fellow-man.
В В В В В В В В В В В В For he who joy on others' paths has thrown,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Will find there's some left over for his own;
В В В В В В В В В В В В And he who leads his brother toward the sky,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Will in the journey bring himself more nigh.

В В В В В В В В В В В В And what I see and think, in my own way,
В В В В В В В В В В В В I'll tell to you, Old Calendar, each day;
В В В В В В В В В В В В And if I choose to do the same in rhyme,
В В В В В В В В В В В В What jury would convict me of a crime?
В В В В В В В В В В В В For every one, from palaces to attics,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Has caught, some time or other, The Rhythmatics.



В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *


В В В В [From Arthur Selwyn's Note-book.]

В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Still through The City I ponder,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Still do I wonder and wander.
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В City - unconscious descendant
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Of olden-time cities resplendent!
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Child of rich forefathers hoary,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Clad in their gloom and their glory! - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В Dream I of you in the rich, mellow past,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Throbbing with life, and with Death overcast.

В В В В В В В В В В В В Thebes - not to you, crushed and ghastly and dumb,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Even the wreck-loving Ivy will come!
В В В В В В В В В В В В Where stood your hundred broad, world-famous gates,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Now a black Arab for charity waits.
В В В В В В В В В В В В Not like this City - metropolis bold - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В Where the whole world brings its goods and its gold!

В В В В В В В В В В В В Babylon - here the queen's gardens climbed high,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Painting their flowers on the blue of the sky:



В В В В В В В В В В В В This is where sinners, one asinine hour,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Thought they could travel to Heaven by tower.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (How like some sinners to-day, whose desires
В В В В В В В В В В В В Mount by the way of their greed-builded spires!)

В В В В В В В В В В В В Troy - of rare riches and valor possessed,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Ruined fore'er by one beautiful guest - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В (Here many Helens, though less of renown,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Do for some men what she did for a town!)

В В В В В В В В В В В В Wondrous Palmyra, whose island of green,
В В В В В В В В В В В В 'Mid the bleak sand, reared the beautiful queen
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (Sweet-faced Zenobia, peerless
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Proud in her virtue, and fearless)
В В В В В В В В В В В В In this metropolis, virtuously grand,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Many a queen is a joy to the land!

В В В В В В В В В В В В Tyre - the huge pillars that groaned under thee,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Rest in the depths of a desolate sea;
В В В В В В В В В В В В Long may it be ere the spray's salted showers
В В В В В В В В В В В В Foam o'er the walls of this city of ours!

В В В В В В В В В В В В Mound-men's vast cities, whose graves we accost,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Even your names are in ruins - and lost.
В В В В В В В В В В В В What if, some time when this nation is nought,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Vainly our names in our graves should be sought!

В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *

В В В В В В В В В В В В Cities that yet are to flourish,
В В В В В В В В В В В В That the rich Future must nourish!
В В В В В В В В В В В В Where will you take up your stations - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В Where set your massive foundations?
В В В В В В В В В В В В Where are the slumbering meadows,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Dreaming of clouds through their shadows,
В В В В В В В В В В В В That by rough wheels rudely shaken,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Into new life shall awaken?
В В В В В В В В В В В В Harbors that placidly float
В В В В В В В В В В В В Nought but the fisherman's boat,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Think you of fleets that shall lie
В В В В В В В В В В В В Under the blue of your sky;
В В В В В В В В В В В В When shall be built on your land
В В В В В В В В В В В В Palaces wealthily grand;
В В В В В В В В В В В В When in your face from tall spires
В В В В В В В В В В В В Gleam the electrical fires?
В В В В В В В В В В В В Cities that yet are to be,
В В В В В В В В В В В В You are not phantoms to me!
В В В В В В В В В В В В You are as certain and sure
В В В В В В В В В В В В As that Old Time shall endure.

В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *

В В В В В В В В В В В В Stars in the distant, mysterious sky,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Flashing and flaming and dancing on high,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Each is an earth to its millions,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Each has its domes and pavilions.
В В В В В В В В В В В В Cities, I see you - by reasoning led - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В On the great map with blue leaves overhead.
В В В В В В В В В В В В Seaport and lakeport and rich inland town,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Capital city, and village of brown;
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Thanking the prairie-food-givers,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Strung on the winding star-rivers.
В В В В В В В В В В В В Earths that can signal to earths, every one,
В В В В В В В В В В В В With the bright torches you stole from the sun,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Each on its surface has strown
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Cities and towns of its own,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Fraught with their crimes and their graces,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Full of mysterious places.
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В They are no myths unto me - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Clearly their outlines I see;
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Millions of towns I descry
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Hanging o'er me from the sky.

В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *

В В В В В В В В В В В В Still through the paths of the town,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Dreaming, I walk up and down.
В В В В В В В В В В В В Is it so much of a wonder - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В Part of this whole, yet asunder,
В В В В В В В В В В В В I in this throng, and I only - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В That I am wretched and lonely?
В В В В В В В В В В В В Loneliness - loneliness ever - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В Leaving me utterly, never!
В В В В В В В В В В В В Yes, I am part of this ocean
В В В В В В В В В В В В Of matter and mind and emotion;
В В В В В В В В В В В В Yet how entirely apart,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Severed in mind and in heart!



В В В В [From Farmer Harrington's Calendar.]

В В В В SEPTEMBER 25, 18 - .

В В В В В В В В В В В В Wealth - wealth - wealth - wealth! I never had been led,
В В В В В В В В В В В В From all I'd thought and dreamed and heard and read,
В В В В В В В В В В В В To think so much wealth, in whatever while,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Could be raked up into one shining pile!
В В В В В В В В В В В В Not long ago, a hundred dollars clear,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Big as a hay-stack would to me appear.
В В В В В В В В В В В В When first a thousand dollars made me smile,
В В В В В В В В В В В В I sympathized with Croesus quite a while;
В В В В В В В В В В В В But looking round here makes me feel the same
В В В В В В В В В В В В As if I hadn't a nickel to my name!

В В В В В В В В В В В В Wealth - wealth! why, every acre I behold
В В В В В В В В В В В В Has cost a mine of Californi' gold!
В В В В В В В В В В В В The very ground one building here might fill
В В В В В В В В В В В В Would almost buy the town of Tompkins Hill!

В В В В В В В В В В В В There isn't a house my scrutiny has crossed
В В В В В В В В В В В В But catches several figures in its cost;
В В В В В В В В В В В В And when your eyes into the parlor go
В В В В В В В В В В В В ('Mongst things they leave the curtains up to show),
В В В В В В В В В В В В And see the carpets, rugs, and draperies rich,
В В В В В В В В В В В В That twine ten dollars into every stitch,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And view great pictures that such prices hold
В В В В В В В В В В В В As if the painter's brush were dipped in gold;

В В В В В В В В В В В В And when along the roads great buggies glide,
В В В В В В В В В В В В With covers on, and rich-dressed folks inside,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And up on top a man to drive the team - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В As fat as any cat brought up on cream
В В В В В В В В В В В В (Man and team both), the driver dressed as gay
В В В В В В В В В В В В As if he meant to marry that same day,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Or wed his boss's daughter that same night
В В В В В В В В В В В В (Which some consider as the coachman's right,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And think it's understood, when he engages,
В В В В В В В В В В В В A daughter should be thrown in with his wages),
В В В В В В В В В В В В When even the horses, as so many do,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Wear jewelry that cost a farm or two,
В В В В В В В В В В В В You wonder in what tree-top grew the cash
В В В В В В В В В В В В To buy so much reality and trash!

В В В В В В В В В В В В Wealth - wealth - wealth - wealth! the very corner stores
В В В В В В В В В В В В Are gold-mines from the ceilings to the floors!
В В В В В В В В В В В В The shop we thought would ruin Cousin Phil,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Because 'twas over-large for Tompkins Hill,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Would, in the small vest-pocket, lose its way,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Of one man's place I wandered through to-day!

В В В В В В В В В В В В And then the banks - a hundred on one street - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В As full of money as an egg of meat
В В В В В В В В В В В В (Although one never knows beyond a doubt
В В В В В В В В В В В В What colored chickens they'll be hatching out);

В В В В В В В В В В В В And then the churches - elegant to view - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В An independent fortune in each pew.
В В В В В В В В В В В В One window-pane in one big church that's here
В В В В В В В В В В В В Cost more than our old preacher made per year!
В В В В В В В В В В В В (A city pastor's salary, I declare,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Would keep him all his life, with cash to spare,
В В В В В В В В В В В В A-preaching in that little house of wood,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Holding his hearers' eyes in all he could,
В В В В В В В В В В В В With rolling meadows and green trees in view,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And fresh-complexioned streamlets wandering through);

В В В В В В В В В В В В And then the rich school-houses in this town,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Where children can be taught up-stairs and down,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Swifter (if not so thorough), I suppose,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Than in the small log school-house, where I rose
В В В В В В В В В В В В From Numeration to the Rule of Three,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And had Irregular Verbs whipped into me;

В В В В В В В В В В В В And then the railroad stations, where, each day,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Fortunes on wheels rush in and drive away;
В В В В В В В В В В В В And then the steamboats paddling up and down - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В Towns swimming on their way from town to town;

В В В В В В В В В В В В And then the ladies, in both street and store,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Done up in silks and satins, spangled o'er
В В В В В В В В В В В В As if it had rained diamonds for an hour,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And they had gone and stood out in the shower;

В В В В В В В В В В В В And then the rich and idle-houred young men - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В The rising generation's "Upper 10"
В В В В В В В В В В В В (With the "1" left off), who each day, no doubt,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Spend twice as much as all my "setting out,"
В В В В В В В В В В В В When Father said, "The family craft is full;
В В В В В В В В В В В В Launch your own craft and show us how to pull."

В В В В В В В В В В В В I often think, when past a dandy glides,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Throwing his (father's) money on all sides,
В В В В В В В В В В В В And peeking under each young lady's veil,
В В В В В В В В В В В В As if he'd bought her at a mortgage sale,
В В В В В В В В В В В В How shrewd it was of him, right on the start,
В В В В В В В В В В В В To have a father who was rich and smart!
В В В В В В В В В В В В (Folks often pride themselves much, by-the-way,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Because their parents greater were than they.)

В В В В В В В В В В В В Walking to-day along Fifth Avenue,
В В В В В В В В В В В В A slip of paper on the sidewalk flew
В В В В В В В В В В В В Before my eyes - some one the same had dropped;
В В В В В В В В В В В В I reached my hand down for it and it stopped.
В В В В В В В В В В В В I picked it up - the reading on't was queer;
В В В В В В В В В В В В I think, perhaps, I'll paste it right in here.
meaning:
William McKendree Carleton's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Wealth."...
